_comment: Shared parity cases for the syntax highlighter. BOTH the author-time Python tool (tools/highlight_code.py, checked by tests/test_highlight_parity.py) and the runtime JS diff highlighter (cmhHighlightCode in assets/js/26-highlight.js, checked by tests/57-highlight-parity.spec.js) must classify each snippet the same way, so the two parallel implementations cannot drift (GH-REGRESS-HIGHLIGHT-PARITY). 'str'/'com'/'kw' list tokens that MUST land in that class; 'notStr' lists lone-quote tokens that must NOT be swallowed as a string (the PR #33 regression class: an UNTERMINATED single quote - a Rust lifetime or a YAML/Ruby apostrophe - must not run to end of line); 'notKw' lists runtime-side tokens that must not be wrapped in a keyword span. Known accepted divergence NOT asserted here: the runtime's compact tokenizer colors a C++ digit separator with PAIRED quotes (1'000'000 -> '000' as a string), which the Python tool avoids via a dedicated char-literal pattern; that is a minor cosmetic difference on a rarely-diffed construct, not the end-of-line over-match #33 fixed.

cases.code: let data = local + val; let s: &'a str = "hi"; // note
SELECT id FROM orders WHERE status = 'paid';
IF 1==1 ECHO Hello & REM a comment
